bullets: Domestic PP broadly lower; supplier gaps narrow under oversupply.
Sabic re-bid June PP down up to 300, deepening short-term pressure.
Platts: NEA PE -25, LDPE -80; SEA LLDPE -55 to 1,300, LDPE -45 to 1,550.
Sabic finalized HDPE Film 1,320 (F04660 1,550); LDPE/LLDPE pending.
Platts: China PVC -70 to 860 CFR; SEA -10 to 810 CFR.

editorial.changedNote: A handful of PP/PE items eased; PVC sideways

summary: NEA and SEA PE fell sharply on weak oil and pre-low-season demand, leaving Vietnam among the lowest.
Sabic finalized HDPE grades while Chinese distributors cut HDPE Film finals up to 100.
Domestic sellers kept lowering imported HDPE/LDPE/LLDPE in a cautious market.
paras: Per Platts, NEA HDPE -25, LLDPE -15, LDPE -80; SEA HDPE -25 to 1,340-1,350, LLDPE -55 to 1,300, mC6 -55 to 1,450, LDPE -45 to 1,550 (Vietnam near regional lows). Another index showed NEA HDPE -10-20, SEA -30-40, LDPE NEA -80-100.
PE fell on weak oil and the coming mid-year low season; SEA trading stayed sparse.
Sabic finalized late-June HDPE ex-Singapore: common Film 1,320, F04660 1,550, blow B5428 1,420, B5429 1,360, injection M200056 1,600 (100 above offer). LDPE/LLDPE not yet final.
Chinese prices kept falling: BASF China HDPE finalized 1,250 (up to 100 below early-week offer); STL HDPE Film finalized 1,280 (20-30 below offer).
Domestically, Chinese 7042 commonly 34,500-35,500; Sinopec HDPE blow 5502 at 38,500; LLDPE 1220F at 36,000.
Qamar F1 finalized 39,500 (Hung Yen), cheapest; Chinese/US/Russian HDPE 38,000-39,000. LDPE premium 49,000-50,000, US-origin 47,000-48,000.

summary: Domestic PP fell broadly with shrinking supplier gaps under oversupply and cheap Chinese imports.
Sabic shocked the market by cutting re-bid June PP up to 300.
Asian PP kept easing on weak oil, soft sentiment and rising Chinese stocks.
paras: On May 29 in the South, domestic PP yarn offers spread widely: NSRP 114G and BSR T3034 commonly 35,900-36,500, some T3034 at 37,000; Hyosung F501 ~36,700; LSP P400S at 38,500. Cheapest was Chinese yarn ~35,000.
In the North, Chinese PP yarn T03 at 34,500 and copo K8003 at 38,000 - near market lows.
Many firms cut PP 114G to 35,500-35,600; gaps narrowed under competition. Off-grade copo to 34,500 (down 500).
Oversupply returned as distributors faced cash-flow, debt and container-storage pressure, selling below market.
Sabic still had not finalized June PP. On May 28 it re-bid: yarn/injection 1,200 (down 300), copo 1,230 (down 310-320), random 1,600 (down 40) - a shock.
Per Platts, NEA PP yarn/injection/BOPP flat, IPP Film -10, block copo -50; SEA all -20 (yarn/injection 1,270, BOPP 1,290, IPP/block copo 1,320). Chinese stocks rose; SEA quiet around Hari Raya Haji.

summary: Asian PVC fell across regions on weak oil cost support, high stocks and soft demand.
SEA buyers stayed hesitant, drawing down earlier-hoarded inventory.
Domestic sellers cut American and Chinese imports; AGC/TPC Vina held June rates.
paras: Per Platts, China PVC -70 to 860 CFR; SEA -10 to 810 CFR; NEA and SEA both -20 (SEA 800-850); India -30.
Optimism on US-Iran talks and a possible Hormuz reopening cut oil prices, eroding oil-based PVC cost support. China inventory high; run rate ~69.85% (up 0.45 pt).
SEA buyers, holding earlier-hoarded stock, bought small.
In Vietnam, sellers cut Shintech SE950/SE1000 to 24,500-25,000. Northern sellers finalized Chinese SG-5 to 22,000-22,100; Chinese oil-based to 24,300-24,500. Trading stayed slow.
